cross_scene_arcs.title: Descent into Professional and Moral Bankruptcy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bruce's unchecked ego and disdain for journalistic integrity culminate in his public humiliation and eventual firing, marking the collapse of his professional identity and self-worth.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bruce abuses his broadcast platform for mockery and personal gratification, rejecting ethical responsibility.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bruce is physically ejected from the news station, unemployed and disgraced, with no clear future.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Bruce's need for validation remains unmet, and his emotional instability sets the stage for a transformative crisis.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Awakening and Acceptance of Divine Power
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bruce transitions from disbelief and denial to reluctant acceptance of supernatural powers, establishing the framework for his godlike abilities and the ethical boundaries he must navigate.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bruce is disillusioned and questioning life's meaning after his professional downfall.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bruce understands the rules of his divine powers—secrecy and non-interference with free will—but has not yet been tested on adherence.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: It remains uncertain whether Bruce will respect the limits of free will when faced with personal desires.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Abuse of Power and Erosion of Relationships
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bruce's growing godlike authority leads to emotional detachment and manipulation, ultimately destroying his relationship with Grace due to his ego, secrecy, and violation of autonomy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bruce begins to see himself as above human limitations, withholding truth from Grace.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Grace leaves Bruce, rejecting his materialism and emotional dishonesty, leaving him isolated.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Bruce fails to recognize his emotional flaws, instead blaming external factors even as he loses the most important relationship in his life.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Reckoning and Redemption
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After losing everything, Bruce undergoes a spiritual transformation, shifting from self-centered ambition to selfless love, culminating in his surrender of divine power and restoration as a humble human.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bruce questions whether love can be controlled and begins doubting his worthiness.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bruce prays selflessly for Grace's happiness, relinquishes godhood, and is reborn as a loving, humble man reunited with her.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: None — this arc resolves fully with Bruce's complete moral and emotional transformation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Vengeance to Benevolent Authority
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bruce initially uses his powers for personal revenge and ego gratification but gradually evolves toward responsible, compassionate use—culminating in healing others and sacrificing his own ambitions.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bruce uses divine power to humiliate attackers and sabotage rivals, reveling in control.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bruce heals Evan, apologizes, and declines the anchor role, choosing moral integrity over status.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Bruce faces the lingering temptation of power but ultimately resists it, proving his internal change.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Accusation to Empowerment: Bruce's Transformation from Atheist to God
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bruce evolves from a state of angry disbelief in God to becoming a literal vessel of divine power. His initial rejection of God as a cruel bureaucrat gives way to undeniable supernatural experiences, culminating in the formal transfer of omnipotence and his public declaration as a divine figure.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bruce views God as a malicious, indifferent force responsible for human suffering.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bruce assumes the role of God, wielding supernatural power and proclaiming himself as divine authority.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The legitimacy and sustainability of Bruce's divine status remain ambiguous—whether he is a true successor or a flawed imitator.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Corrupting Influence of Power and the Erosion of Love
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: As Bruce ascends to divine power, his relationship with Grace deteriorates due to his growing ego, unilateral decisions, and emotional detachment. Despite divine interventions to guide reconciliation, Grace ultimately chooses freedom from love over reunion, revealing the limits of power in matters of the heart.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bruce and Grace's relationship is strained by his spiritual transformation and growing self-importance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Grace forgets her love for Bruce through divine intervention, achieving emotional release while Bruce bears the pain of loss alone.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The moral cost of erasing love as a solution to heartbreak remains unaddressed—whether it is mercy or tragedy.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Divine Responsibility vs. Human Ego: The Crisis of Stewardship
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bruce's use of divine power shifts from benevolent intervention to ego-driven manipulation, prompting God to intervene and teach him that true divinity lies in restraint, empathy, and respect for free will. The arc traces Bruce's moral education through failure and revelation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bruce ignores prayers and uses power for personal amusement, violating the spirit of divine stewardship.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bruce learns that fulfilling desires without wisdom causes suffering, and he must embrace personal responsibility over omnipotent control.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Bruce still fears his own inadequacy without divine support, suggesting incomplete internalization of the lesson.

cross_scene_arcs.title: God's Hidden Guidance: Subtle Interventions in a Chaotic World
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: God operates through indirect, symbolic acts—such as prison wall holes, tree carvings, and answering prayers through Bruce—to guide humanity without overt interference. These moments reveal a divine strategy rooted in subtlety, patience, and respect for free will.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: God restores balance by reclaiming authority and guiding Bruce back to humility, demonstrating ultimate control through quiet influence.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The effectiveness and interpretability of God's subtle signs remain uncertain—many may dismiss them as coincidence.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Paradox of Divine Power: Omnipotence and Powerlessness
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: This arc explores the contradiction at the heart of divinity: God is all-powerful yet constrained by love and free will. Bruce's journey mirrors this paradox—he gains total power but loses control over love, while God demonstrates that true strength lies in restraint and suffering alongside humanity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Bruce accuses God of cruelty for allowing suffering, assuming omnipotence implies direct intervention.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bruce understands that divine power includes the choice not to act, and that meaning emerges from struggle, not miracles.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The mystery of God's ways remains intact—humans cannot fully comprehend why some prayers are answered and others are not.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fracturing of Shared Values
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Grace's relationship with Bruce deteriorates as their core values diverge—hers rooted in compassion, service, and emotional stability, his in ambition, self-fulfillment, and later, supernatural transformation. Early ideological differences over altruism and realism foreshadow deeper rifts that culminate in emotional abandonment.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Grace and Bruce hold differing but coexisting worldviews, with Grace committed to community and Bruce skeptical of sacrifice.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Grace is emotionally shattered after Bruce rejects her reassurance and prioritizes career over their relationship, marking a point of no return in their emotional connection.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The imbalance in emotional labor and coping mechanisms—Grace's nurturing versus Bruce's self-absorption—remains unaddressed, leaving their bond irreparably broken.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Loss and Reclamation of Bodily Autonomy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Grace experiences a series of violations and unexplained physical transformations that strip her of control over her body, beginning with Bruce's supernatural manipulation and culminating in a miraculous restoration she interprets as divine. Her body becomes a battleground between external control and personal agency.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Grace is subjected to a non-consensual, magically induced orgasm, marking the first explicit violation of her bodily autonomy by Bruce.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Her body returns to normal after prayer, which she interprets as a miracle, restoring her sense of self but highlighting Bruce's emotional detachment from her suffering.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Bruce's role in causing or enabling the transformations remains ambiguous, leaving Grace's long-term safety and agency uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Hope to Heartbreak: The Proposal That Wasn't
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Grace builds emotional momentum toward a hoped-for marriage proposal, only to have her expectations crushed when Bruce reveals the evening was about his career. This betrayal catalyzes her rejection of his materialism and marks the beginning of her emotional independence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Grace anticipates a marriage proposal, interpreting Bruce's plans as a romantic milestone after his professional success.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She publicly ends the relationship, rejecting Bruce and his gifts, symbolizing her break from emotional dependency and false promises.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Grace has not yet defined her new identity beyond rejection, leaving her future goals undefined despite her hard-won clarity.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Supernatural Pressures and Spiritual Dilemmas
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After breaking up with Bruce, Grace is bombarded with surreal signs—a personalized song, mysterious photos, and a divine cloud message—all urging her to forgive him. These events blur the line between spiritual guidance and manipulation, forcing her to assert her autonomy against metaphysical pressure.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Grace begins to question reality when a radio song narrates her private relationship, introducing the idea of a scripted or observed life.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She resists supernatural and emotional coercion by saying 'No' to Bruce's plea, affirming her choice despite ambiguous divine signals.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The origin of the signs—whether divine, psychological, or engineered by Bruce—remains unknown, leaving Grace's spiritual understanding unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Asymmetry of Letting Go
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: While Grace pleads with God to stop loving Bruce, Bruce learns she still prays for him, triggering guilt and introspection. Their emotional arcs converge in a moment of spiritual exchange: her release is his burden, illustrating a profound asymmetry in their healing.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Grace is trapped in emotional anguish, unable to move on despite her efforts to maintain distance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She experiences sudden release as Bruce absorbs her pain, achieving inner peace while he takes on her suffering.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Grace remains unaware of the cost of her healing—Bruce now carries her pain—creating a silent, unbalanced emotional debt between them.
